body_txt:
A character, often a child or teenager and almost Always Female and on the political left, who cares deeply about all worthy causes. She wants to protect the environment from polluters, prove that women aren't inferior to men , and Free the Frogs . If she's the main character of the show, she spends a lot of time struggling with her idealism and whether it is too unrealistic. Soap Box Sadie is usually a post-hippie , the type of girl who would have been a hippie had she not been born in the wrong decade. Sometimes she's updated to the more recent counter-cultures of the time like emos, goths, hipsters, etc. Around the turn of The New '10s , the gender-neutral terms "Social Justice Warrior" and "Woke" started being used for a type of Sadie concerned with issues of social equality and diversity (i.e. race, religion, gender, sexuality, disability, etc). It refers to a type of progressive who advocates for social reform in a detrimental way : getting outraged at every petty grievance, expressing hatred and bigotry towards groups they perceive to be "privileged", speaking OVER marginalized groups and presuming to know what issues are important to them, and caring more about sounding morally superior than doing anything that actually helps. The terms can also be used as an insult (and usually are an insult when used online by an Internet Jerk ), intended to get people to shut up even when the "social justice warrior" actually has a point and isn't expressing it in an unreasonable manner. In most shows, Sadie exists to teach children Anvilicious lessons about the importance of the environment, often initiating a Nature Clean-Up ,
feminism, standing up for one's beliefs, and so on. Depending on the writer's own moral stances and opinions, she'll often be portrayed as Incorruptible Pure Pureness incarnate and an All-Loving Hero for the alienated, a flawed individual trying her best to improve the world (but sometimes in an ultimately detriminal or misguided way), or The Fundamentalist of a different stripe who revels in forcing her beliefs down others' throats. Compare Granola Girl , Literal Soapbox Speech , Straw Feminist , Straw Vegetarian , Strawman Political , Author Tract , and The Troublemaker . Very likely to join or set up an Asinine Activist Group . Special bonus if they actually get on a soapbox . Works made in The New '10s and The New '20s will almost always give them Nonconformist Dyed Hair . May overlap with Goody Two-Shoes . There's also the black equivalent of this trope called Malcolm Xerox . See Former Teen Rebel for those who have outgrown this. noreallife
